title: Arc section classification
current_status: ~64% of phrases fall back to gat_development; alap, peak, and resolution are sparse.
research_directions: Expert labeling: Get 200–300 phrases labeled for arc section by an expert.
Temporal features: Estimate position_ratio from original recording timestamps.
ML model: Train classifier on phrase_density, contour_direction, pitch_histogram, position_ratio.
Literature review: Refine heuristics with Napier (2019) proportions and Saraga structural annotations.
key_findings: Napier 2019 (235 ālāp examples): upper-tonic climax at 55% of whole performance, rhythmic intensification at 70%; Saraga provides section boundaries for 75 recordings and phrases for 53.

arc_rules.peak_threshold: 0.55 < position_ratio < 0.75 AND high density + ascending contour

rationale: Directly enables section-aware fusion and better arc_profile adherence.

title: Ornament detection
current_status: F1 ~0.02; many FPs and low recall.
research_directions: ROD vs. detector comparison: Tune thresholds on ROD disagreements.
Per-type calibration: Separate models/rules for gamak, meend, kan, murki.
ML approach: Train on ROD + f0 contours (ED-TCN baseline already 90% F1).
key_findings: ROD 2025: 212 recordings (4.08 h), 6 ornaments (kaṇ, mīnd, murkī, nyās, andolan, gamak), expert active annotation, ED-TCN F1 90.06% (collar), generalizes to concerts at 67.59% after fine-tune.
rationale: Enables reliable ornament density for recommender and phrase characterization.

title: Phrase intent tags
current_status: No phrases have intent_tags; fusion uses request-level intent only.
research_directions: Derived tags from density, contour, energy, pitch range.
Corpus analysis: Map expert descriptions (alap=meditative, taan=energetic) to metadata.
key_findings: Saraga phrases + Napier energy build-up provide direct mapping; simple rule-based bootstrap possible immediately.
rationale: Quick win for per-phrase fusion selection and diversity.

title: Raga–genre tempo limits
research_directions: Structured extraction: Add tempo_ceiling_bpm, tempo_floor_bpm, best_bpm_range per raga–genre.
key_findings: Traditional: vilambit 30-70, madhya 70-180, drut 180-350 BPM. Grave ragas (Darbari) <60 BPM for andolan; fusion (Charanjit Singh disco 120-140 BPM) possible with ornament adaptation.
rationale: Hard constraints prevent character loss in production.

title: Style affinity validation
current_status: 74% non-default scores from LLM; unvalidated by listening.
research_directions: ABX listening tests on recommended pairs.
key_findings: Existing fusion discography (Shankar-Jaikishan 1968 jazz, Charanjit Singh 1982 disco, Anoushka Shankar downtempo) provides ground-truth examples for calibration.

title: Thin-raga expansion
research_directions: Needs analysis for ragas below 100 phrases.
key_findings: Saraga Hindustani: 108 recordings, 61 ragas, 53 with manual phrase annotations, 75 with structural sections—direct source for expansion.
rationale: Fixes coverage gaps for balanced generation.

title: Quality score calibration
key_findings: Correlate with ROD/Saraga expert annotations for ground truth.

title: Cross-raga compatibility
key_findings: Same thaat = high compatibility (0.9); shared vadi/samvadi or ≥4 swaras = medium (0.6-0.8). Bhatkhande 10 thaats provide clean basis.
